description: evtx-to-csv enrichment signature library. Edit and save, then click Refresh in the app.

patterns.name: ps_encoded_command
patterns.description: PowerShell launched with -EncodedCommand/-enc: Base64-encodes the payload to hide the actual command from casual inspection and bypass script-level logging.

patterns.name: ps_invoke_expression
patterns.description: Invoke-Expression (IEX) executes an arbitrary string as code at runtime. Hallmark of fileless malware.

patterns.name: ps_download_cradle
patterns.description: Remote script download via WebClient/DownloadString or Invoke-WebRequest. Pulls attacker code directly into memory.

patterns.name: ps_bits_transfer
patterns.description: BITS abused for file download/upload. BITS transfers survive reboots and run as a background service.

patterns.name: ps_execution_bypass
patterns.description: PowerShell execution policy bypass or hidden-window flags. Used to suppress restrictions and avoid visible console windows.

patterns.name: ps_reflection_load
patterns.description: Reflective assembly loading injects a .NET DLL directly into memory without touching disk.

patterns.name: ps_remoting
patterns.description: PowerShell remoting used with a -ComputerName target — indicates lateral movement attempt via WinRM.

patterns.name: ps_network_socket
patterns.description: Direct use of .NET network socket classes. Indicates a custom C2 channel or data exfiltration routine.

patterns.name: ps_amsi_bypass
patterns.description: AMSI bypass attempt detected. Attackers patch amsi.dll in memory to prevent real-time scanning.

patterns.name: ps_decompression
patterns.description: PowerShell using Gzip/Deflate decompression. Often used to hide large scripts or second-stage payloads.

patterns.name: ps_amsi_bypass_reflection
patterns.description: Advanced AMSI bypass using reflection to access AmsiUtils. Targets the internal .NET implementation of AMSI.

patterns.name: ps_marshal_memory_manipulation
patterns.description: PowerShell using System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal for direct memory access. Indicates shellcode injection or memory exploitation.

patterns.name: lolbin_certutil_download
patterns.description: certutil.exe used to download a remote file — a well-known LOLBin abuse technique.

patterns.name: lolbin_mshta
patterns.description: mshta.exe executing an HTA file or remote URL. MSHTA runs HTML Applications with full scripting privileges.

patterns.name: lolbin_regsvr32_squiblydoo
patterns.description: Squiblydoo attack: regsvr32.exe used to execute a remote COM scriptlet. Bypasses AppLocker.

patterns.name: lolbin_rundll32_minidump
patterns.description: rundll32.exe invoking comsvcs.dll MiniDump — used to dump LSASS process memory.

patterns.name: lolbin_wmic_exec
patterns.description: WMIC used for process creation or remote execution without spawning cmd.exe directly.

patterns.name: lolbin_cscript_wscript
patterns.description: CScript/WScript executing a script file. Frequently used to run .vbs/.js droppers.

patterns.name: lolbin_installutil
patterns.description: InstallUtil.exe abused to execute arbitrary .NET code. Bypasses application whitelisting.

patterns.name: lolbin_cmstp
patterns.description: CMSTP.exe exploited to execute arbitrary DLLs or COM scriptlets. Bypasses UAC.

patterns.name: lolbin_msiexec_remote
patterns.description: msiexec.exe used to install a package from a remote URL. Allows execution of arbitrary code.

patterns.name: lolbin_psexec
patterns.description: PsExec used for remote command execution. Frequently used by ransomware operators.

patterns.name: lolbin_pcalua_execution
patterns.description: Program Compatibility Assistant (pcalua.exe) used to proxy execution.

patterns.name: lolbin_file_extraction
patterns.description: Native Windows tools (makecab, expand, extrac32) used for file extraction or cabinet creation.

patterns.name: defender_tamper
patterns.description: Windows Defender configuration modified. Adding exclusion paths or disabling real-time monitoring.

patterns.name: shadow_copy_delete
patterns.description: Shadow copy/backup deletion detected. Executed immediately before ransomware encryption.

patterns.name: event_log_clear_cmd
patterns.description: Windows event log cleared via wevtutil or PowerShell. Destroying forensic evidence.

patterns.name: bcdedit_recovery_disabled
patterns.description: bcdedit used to disable Windows recovery. Prevents booting into recovery mode.

patterns.name: credential_dump_tool
patterns.description: Known credential dumping tool detected: Mimikatz, WCE, fgdump, pwdump, gsecdump, or LSASS dump.

patterns.name: credential_dump_lsass
patterns.description: Direct LSASS process dump detected. Provides all logged-on user credential hashes.

patterns.name: remote_access_tool
patterns.description: Unauthorized remote access tool detected (AnyDesk, ngrok, netcat, chisel, Cobalt Strike).

patterns.name: wmi_persistence_eventing
patterns.description: WMI event subscription created. Used to trigger malicious code when system events occur.

patterns.name: persistence_run_key
patterns.description: Registry Run/RunOnce key modification. Program executes at every user login.

patterns.name: persistence_schtask_create
patterns.description: Scheduled task created via schtasks /create. Malware survives reboots and re-executes.

patterns.name: persistence_service_create
patterns.description: Windows service created or reconfigured. Malicious services for persistent SYSTEM-level execution.

patterns.name: persistence_winlogon_shell_userinit
patterns.description: Winlogon Shell or Userinit registry key modified. Provides reliable persistence at login.

patterns.name: persistence_ifeo_debugger
patterns.description: Image File Execution Options (IFEO) debugger hijack. Runs malware whenever a targeted app launches.

patterns.name: persistence_appinit_dlls
patterns.description: Persistence via AppInit_DLLs registry key. Loads DLL into every process linking with User32.dll.

patterns.name: persistence_startup_folder
patterns.description: File copied to the Windows Startup folder to run automatically at login.

patterns.name: user_account_creation
patterns.description: New local user account created via command line. Fallback access point.

patterns.name: user_added_to_admin_group
patterns.description: User added to the local Administrators group. Grants full control over the system.

patterns.name: firewall_disabled
patterns.description: Windows Firewall disabled via netsh. Allows unrestricted network traffic.

patterns.name: rdp_enabled_registry
patterns.description: Remote Desktop (RDP) enabled via registry. Facilitates remote interaction.

patterns.name: service_stop_security
patterns.description: Security-related service stopped via net stop. Targets Defender, SENSE, or Firewall.

patterns.name: recon_whoami
patterns.description: whoami executed — confirms the compromised user account and its privilege level.

patterns.name: recon_net_user_domain
patterns.description: net user /domain or net group — domain user/group enumeration.

patterns.name: recon_systeminfo
patterns.description: systeminfo executed — gathers OS version, patch level, and hotfixes.

patterns.name: recon_network_discovery
patterns.description: Network configuration discovery (arp, route, netstat, ipconfig).

patterns.name: recon_tasklist
patterns.description: tasklist or Get-Process used to enumerate running processes.

patterns.name: recon_password_search
patterns.description: Searching for passwords in files via findstr. Look for plaintext credentials.

patterns.name: data_staging_archiving
patterns.description: Data staging using archive tools (7z, rar, zip). Indicates preparation for exfiltration.

patterns.name: heuristic_base64_in_command
patterns.description: Long Base64 string detected in a command field. Suggests obfuscated payload delivery.

patterns.name: heuristic_temp_path_execution
patterns.description: Executable running from Temp, AppData, or Downloads path.

patterns.name: heuristic_double_extension
patterns.description: File with double extension detected (e.g. invoice.pdf.exe). Masquerading technique.

patterns.name: lolbin_cmd_chaining
patterns.description: cmd.exe /c or /r used for command chaining or proxy execution.

patterns.name: lolbin_dotnet_proxies
patterns.description: RegAsm or RegSvcs used to proxy execution of arbitrary .NET code.

patterns.name: lolbin_odbcconf
patterns.description: odbcconf.exe used to execute a DLL or script. Less common LOLBin.

patterns.name: lolbin_xwizard
patterns.description: xwizard.exe used for arbitrary DLL loading to bypass security controls.
